🇧🇷 BRAZILIAN GP RANKINGS

✍️ The 2026 MotoGP rankings are based on an analytical methodology developed by @deltadata, structured around predefined performance scales designed to measure each rider’s performance as accurately as possible. The model aims to isolate external factors unrelated to riding ability, focusing the evaluation on the rider’s capacity to achieve results relative to the estimated performance level of their motorcycle. The analysis is strictly limited to on-track results obtained throughout the season.

Evaluation Criteria:

Combined session analysis with weighted distribution: qualifying (10%), sprint race (25%), and main race (65%).

An approximate deduction of 0.4 points in the rating for every tenth of a second per lap difference. This coefficient may vary depending on specific criteria, such as whether the rider competes on a factory or satellite motorcycle.

Continuous performance assessment throughout the season.

Specific consideration of motorcycle performance in order to isolate the technical factor from the rider’s contribution.

Analysis of each race start and positions gained relative to starting grid position.

Grands Prix contested by riders with evident physical limitations are excluded from evaluation.

Excluded Variables:

Contextual factors (rookie status, manufacturer changes, adaptation processes, etc.).

These are the season ratings for the 2025 MotoGP riders.

The MotoGP 2025 Rankings are based on an analytical methodology developed by @deltadata, built on predefined scales designed to measure each rider’s true on-track performance. The model aims to isolate the human factor by accounting for bike performance, focusing the evaluation on the rider and limiting the analysis strictly to on-track results throughout the season.

Evaluation criteria:
- Combined analysis of qualifying, sprint, and race.
- Continuous assessment over the course of the season.
- Specific consideration of bike performance to isolate the technical factor.

Excluded variables
- Contextual factors (rookie status, manufacturer changes, etc.).
- Grands Prix contested with an evident physical impairment.

These are the riders who are not yet guaranteed to stay in MotoGP regardless of their contract.

Honda’s situation is the one that can change the most, as 3 of its 4 riders are still without contract (although Zarco’s continuity is almost certain).

Morbidelli also has a favorable situation and the only threat he had was Acosta who seems to remain with KTM until the end of his contract.

Yamaha despite having its 4 riders confirmed for 2026 is a question mark since only Fabio is getting results for the Japanese brand.

And finally we have the Moto2 riders Moreira, who has sounded strongly for Honda and Yamaha and on the other hand Manu Gonzalez who already got on the Aprilia, but the fact that the 4 riders are still with the Noale brand and their passport makes it difficult for him to move up to the premier class.
